title: Selected CUDA symbols use compact semantic aliases
summary: Selected CUDA handles and complex helpers use compact semantic spellings, with every alias retaining CUDA seafoam provenance.
details: The exact mappings are cudaStream_t -> Stream, cudaGraph_t -> Graph, cudaGraphExec_t -> GraphExec, cuCabsf -> abs, and cuConjf -> conj.
Exact semantic aliases run before generic cuda/cu prefix conceal, avoiding mechanically shortened spellings such as Stream_t, Graph_t, GraphExec_t, Cabsf, and Conjf.
The mappings apply in raw code and structured declaration rendering; handle types also remain consistent in parameters and moved C-style returns.
Exact identifier boundaries prevent partial rewrites inside longer project identifiers.
All displayed aliases remain CUDA seafoam because classification uses the source token before its spelling changes.
Include and include_next directives are excluded from every character-changing frontend pass, so the same strings remain verbatim when they occur in header paths.
CUDA execution-space qualifier spellings remain an open design question rather than being changed as part of these symbol aliases.
rationale: Stream, Graph, and GraphExec communicate handle roles without the runtime prefix or C typedef suffix.
abs and conj communicate the mathematical operations directly while color retains CUDA origin.
An explicit table keeps raw expressions, overlays, types, parameters, and return movement from drifting.
alternatives: Hide only the cuda/cu prefix and show Stream_t, Graph_t, GraphExec_t, Cabsf, and Conjf; rejected by the owner in favor of semantic spellings.
Remove CUDA provenance after shortening; rejected because the shared classify-before-strip rule treats color as retained information.

provenance.note: Owner explicitly selected cudaStream_t -> Stream, cudaGraph_t -> Graph, cudaGraphExec_t -> GraphExec, cuCabsf -> abs, and cuConjf -> conj, with CUDA color retained.

provenance.reference: lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/parse.lua exact CUDA alias tables and aliases.lua::cuda_idents
provenance.note: Structured and raw rendering consume shared exact mappings before generic prefix removal.

evidence.claim: Raw expressions, overlays, parameters, moved returns, CUDA highlight groups, Visual reveal, source integrity, and include exclusion are pinned.

questions: How do cudaStream_t, cudaGraph_t, cudaGraphExec_t, cuCabsf, and cuConjf display?
Why is abs still CUDA green?
Are CUDA exact aliases applied inside include paths?

last_verified.method: Owner selections implemented through shared exact tables and verified in focused static and interaction specs.
